prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: C₆H₅NO₂) using the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill at the DFT level, tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, exact four-center integrals, density fitting OFF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three separate SCF calculations were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Fukui indices were derived by finite differences of Mulliken partial charges across the N, N−1, and N+1 electron states.

Key results (per-atom, 1-based index):
  • f⁺ (electrophilic attack susceptibility): C4 (ipso, NO₂-bearing carbon) = +0.49642 [most electrophilic], O1 = +0.47317, O2 = +0.47344
  • f⁻ (nucleophilic attack susceptibility): C5 = +0.10729 [most nucleophilic], C6 = +0.10724
  • Dual descriptor: C4 = +0.51071 (strongly electrophilic), N = −0.47394 (strongly nucleophilic/donor)
  • Charge conservation: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 ✅

The headline reported value is the maximum f⁺ = 0.4964178 at atom 4 (C4, ipso carbon). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No engine warnings were reported (warnings array empty — clean run). Output files: nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json and a Fukui bar chart PNG at the path shown in the result.
